intelligenceLadder.name: Ad Hoc
intelligenceLadder.subtitle: Blind
intelligenceLadder.description: No structured visibility; teams operate on instinct; outcomes reactive.

intelligenceLadder.name: Assigned
intelligenceLadder.subtitle: Reactive
intelligenceLadder.description: Roles named and processes documented but inconsistent in practice.

intelligenceLadder.name: Accountable
intelligenceLadder.subtitle: Diagnostic
intelligenceLadder.description: Structured ownership; standardized motions; outcomes measured and reviewed.

intelligenceLadder.name: Optimised
intelligenceLadder.subtitle: Predictive
intelligenceLadder.description: Variance investigated; motions tuned; predictive signals integrated.

intelligenceLadder.name: Predictive
intelligenceLadder.subtitle: Intelligent
intelligenceLadder.description: Autonomous sense-predict-act-learn loop; humans focus on judgment and exception.

dimensions.label: People
dimensions.description: Who owns the work; role design; skills; training.

dimensions.label: Process
dimensions.description: How work flows; documentation; cadence; governance.

dimensions.label: Technology
dimensions.description: Tools, systems, and integration enabling the work.

nrrBands.label: Strong
nrrBands.description: Healthy expansion engine; PE valuation tailwind.

capabilities.name: NRR Reporting
capabilities.tagline: How the company defines, calculates, and reports NRR.

levers.name: Definition & Scope
levers.description: Single documented NRR definition agreed across functions.
levers.question: Can every NRR conversation anchor in a single, documented, agreed-upon definition — including netting rules, cohort lock, and component scope?

answers.text: No agreed definition — teams use different versions; conversations derail on what 'NRR' even means.
Informal definition exists; one analyst owns it; partial documentation; inconsistent across reports.
Single documented definition owned by Finance / RevOps; followed consistently across all NRR reports.
Definition under change-control; versions tracked; refreshed on cadence with audit trail.
Continuously refined; automated consistency checks; data lineage tracked end-to-end.

levers.name: Component Capture
levers.description: Start, Expansion, Contraction, Churn captured cleanly per period.
levers.question: Can the company capture Starting MRR, Expansion, Contraction, and Churn cleanly per period without manual stitching or reconciliation pain?

answers.text: Components extracted ad-hoc each period via manual spreadsheet exports; heavy reconciliation pain.
One analyst pulls components manually; partial automation; reconciliation issues are common.
Standardized component capture documented and followed; automated extraction from billing / CRM.
Integrated component pipeline; near-real-time capture; variance flagged automatically.
System captures and validates components autonomously; anomalies caught proactively.

levers.name: Calculation Method
levers.description: NRR calculation automated, version-controlled, reproducible.
levers.question: Is the NRR calculation automated, version-controlled, auditable, and reproducible across periods — or does the number change depending on who runs it?

answers.text: Calculated in spreadsheets; method varies by analyst; formula errors common; results not reproducible.
Calculation steps documented but inconsistent in practice; reconciliation issues across periods.
Calculation version-controlled in BI / dbt models; reproducible across periods; team trained.
Calculation audited each cycle; variances investigated; cross-team validation in place.
Continuously refined and outcome-tested; autonomous validation; closed-loop refinement.

levers.name: Granularity
levers.description: NRR sliceable by product, segment, cohort, tenure, geo.
levers.question: Can NRR be sliced by product, segment, cohort, tenure, and geo on demand — with consistent definitions across every cut?

answers.text: NRR available only at company level; no slicing capability.
Some slicing on ad-hoc request; inconsistent definitions across cuts; partial coverage.
Standardized slicing across product, segment, cohort, geo, tenure with consistent definitions.
Self-serve cohort analysis; cross-cut consistency governed; predictive cohorting available.
Natural-language slicing; agentic cohort discovery; system surfaces meaningful cuts.

levers.name: Cadence
levers.description: NRR refreshed at a cadence that makes it actionable.
levers.question: Is NRR refreshed at a cadence that makes it actionable — or is the latest number weeks behind reality?

answers.text: Reported quarterly with weeks of lag; the number feels 'after the fact' by the time it lands.
Monthly reporting but heavy manual prep; lag of days; not actionable in-cycle.
Monthly cadence with standardized prep; automated refresh; reliable monthly delivery.
Weekly / on-demand reporting; near-real-time pipeline; current within a day.
Continuous refresh; live numbers; system flags shifts as they happen.

levers.name: Trust & Distribution
levers.description: NRR trusted at exec and board levels — not disputed at every readout.
levers.question: Is the NRR number trusted at exec and board levels — informing strategic decisions and valuation — or disputed at every readout?

answers.text: Number disputed at every readout; data sources contradictory; reconciliation impossible.
Used internally but not trusted at exec level; reconciled but stakeholders still dispute.
Trusted at department / function level; standardized distribution to leadership.
Trusted at exec level; informs strategic decisions; integrated into operating reviews.
Trusted at board level; basis for valuation; self-serve at all levels with explained variances.

levers.name: Forward-Looking
levers.description: Leading indicators and forecast — not just historical reporting.
levers.question: Does NRR reporting include leading indicators and forecast that flag trajectory before the number moves — or is it strictly backward-looking?

answers.text: Only historical NRR reported; no forward view; no leading indicators captured.
Some forecasts attempted ad hoc based on rep gut-feel; leading indicators partial.
Documented forecast methodology with leading indicators; produced each cycle by Finance / RevOps.
Governed forecast with variance-to-actuals tracking; predictive analytics integrated.
Continuously refined autonomous forecast; ML scenario planning; outcome-tested.

capabilities.name: Revenue Retention
capabilities.tagline: Defending the installed base — preventing churn and contraction.

levers.name: Impact Engine
levers.description: Prove the value an account is getting today.
levers.question: Can the company sense realized value per account, predict where it's slipping, and act on the evidence before renewal?

People.text: No one quantifies value for renewal; relationship-only.
One or two CSMs build value recaps for renewals ad hoc.
Trained CSMs articulate value evidence per account on a cadence.
CSMs coached on value signals; QBR-driven; segmented by tier.
System surfaces value gaps automatically; CSMs handle exceptions.

Process.text: No method to assemble value evidence for renewal.
Ad-hoc value recaps stitched manually each renewal cycle.
Standardized value-realization review on a renewal cadence.
Reviewed, audited, governed across the renewal portfolio.
Process autonomous; system runs value review; humans handle exceptions.

Technology.text: Nothing instrumented for value tracking; spreadsheets and slide decks.
Basic CS tool tracks usage; partial CRM integration.
CS platform with health scores tied to renewal hub.
Integrated stack — CS, CRM, product, billing, support.
AI-native; predictive models flag renewal slippage automatically.

Data.text: No reliable outcome data; finance system only.
Some usage data captured but stale, partial, unjoined.
Joined customer 360 refreshed regularly; powers health.
Near-real-time customer data graph with predictive features.
Data substrate autonomous; quality and lineage governed.

levers.name: Whitespace
levers.description: Surface headroom and risk across the base.
levers.question: Can the company sense renewal risk and adoption gaps, predict where they'll surface, and route the right intervention in time?

People.text: No one watches for renewal risk; surprises at renewal.
Some CSMs flag risk reactively when an account flares up.
Trained CSMs watch risk signals on a cadence; clear escalation.
Risk specialists coached; segmented coverage; outcomes tracked.
Risk team leveraged by system; humans handle complex risk.

Process.text: No risk-scoring method; risk is whatever the CSM feels.
Health score exists in one tool but stale or ignored.
Standardized risk-scoring model with defined triggers.
Risk model governed, audited, A/B-tested; closed-loop.
Risk detection and routing partially autonomous.

Technology.text: No risk tooling; reviewed only when account flares.
Basic CS health score; partial coverage; manual analysis.
Risk-scoring tool runs across base; signals joined and visible.
Risk model current; routes to owners with full context.
AI-native; risk model learns from outcomes; agentic interventions.

Data.text: Risk signal data missing, stale, or unjoined.
Partial signal data; manual joins; stale by the time used.
Joined signal data (usage, sentiment, support); refreshed.
Near-real-time risk signal graph with predictive features.
Data substrate enables autonomous risk detection; lineage governed.

levers.name: Account Plan
levers.description: Plan per account against live signals.
levers.question: Can the company plan retention moves anchored in live signals, and refine plans as account reality evolves?

People.text: No retention plans; everything in the CSM's head.
Some CSMs keep retention plans in shared docs; inconsistent.
CSMs trained to build retention plans tied to live signals.
Plans coached against renewal outcomes; segmented by tier.
System recommends next moves; CSMs make strategic decisions.

Process.text: No template or cadence for retention planning.
Plans in shared docs; stale by renewal time.
Standardized retention plan template, kept current, coached against.
Plans auto-update from live signals; governed cadence.
Plans prescriptive; system suggests moves; humans handle exceptions.

Technology.text: Retention plans in stale Word docs or nothing.
Plans in shared cloud docs; no live data attached.
Plans live in CS platform with live signal references.
Plans integrated with CRM, CS, signals; auto-refresh.
AI-native plan system; agentic suggestions; closed-loop.

Data.text: Plans not anchored in real data; rep notes only.
Plans reference some data manually pulled; stale.
Plans reference current usage, health, outcome data.
Plans pull near-real-time joined data; ML-ready.
Data enables plan automation; quality governed.

levers.name: Plays
levers.description: Run the right play when a signal fires.
levers.question: Can the company detect retention triggers (sponsor change, usage drop, support spike), route the right play, and learn which plays land?

People.text: No retention plays; teams improvise when signals fire.
A few CSMs run save plays; manual triggers; inconsistent.
CSMs trained on named retention plays (save, sponsor change, usage drop).
Specialized owners coached on play execution; outcomes tracked.
System leverages team; humans run exception plays only.

Process.text: No defined retention play library; ad-hoc response.
A few plays documented; manual alerts; uneven application.
Comprehensive retention play library with triggers, owners, SLAs.
Plays governed, audited, A/B-tested.
System detects retention signals, routes plays, refines.

Technology.text: No tooling; retention signals missed or noticed by accident.
Manual alerts; assignments tracked in spreadsheets.
Systems detect retention triggers and route plays with context.
Automated trigger-to-action; outcomes tracked end-to-end.
Agentic workflows initiate retention plays; closed-loop learning.

Data.text: Retention signal data not joined; triggers undetectable.
Triggers detected manually after the fact.
Joined signal data; retention triggers detectable in time.
Near-real-time signal graph with predictive triggering.
System learns which plays land on which retention signals.

levers.name: Execution
levers.description: Execute the motion stage-by-stage.
levers.question: Can the team execute the renewal motion with intelligence flowing through every stage gate — value, fit, risk, commit, close?

People.text: No coaching on renewal motion; CSMs improvise.
Some CSMs follow a method; uneven skill at renewals.
All CSMs coached on stage-gated renewal motion; certified.
Renewal coaching tied to live data; segmented by tier.
System recommends next-best-action per stage; CSMs handle complex saves.

Process.text: No defined renewal motion or stage gates.
Loose renewal stages; data captured inconsistently.
Standardized renewal motion with stage gates; instrumented.
Renewal motion measured end-to-end; A/B-tested; coached against outcomes.
Renewal motion partially autonomous; system runs routine.

Technology.text: No CRM stages for renewals; tracked in spreadsheets.
Basic CRM renewal stages; manual approval and tracking.
CRM / CS / e-sign integrated for renewals end-to-end.
Best-of-breed renewal stack; live forecast feed.
AI-native renewal motion; agentic execution.

Data.text: Renewal pipeline data unreliable or incomplete.
Renewal pipeline data exists but stale or disputed.
Renewal pipeline data trustworthy and current; powers forecast.
Near-real-time renewal pipeline; predictive forecast.
Data substrate for autonomous renewal decisions.

levers.name: Incentives
levers.description: Comp the right outcomes.
levers.question: Is comp tuned by clean attribution and outcome data so reps are paid for the retention behavior that actually moves GRR?

People.text: CSMs unsure how retention behavior maps to comp.
Some retention metrics tied to comp but unclear or disputed.
CSMs trained on comp model; clear line behavior-to-payout.
Retention comp coached against performance; reinforced via deal desk.
Continuous comp tuning from retention outcome data.

Process.text: Comp doesn't reward retention; CSMs focus elsewhere.
Some retention credit exists but partial; attribution disputed.
Comp engineered for GRR, on-time renewals, save rate.
Retention comp governed, audited, integrated with performance management.
Comp model continuously refined from retention outcomes.

Technology.text: Retention comp calculated manually in spreadsheets.
Basic comp tool; partial integration with CS metrics.
Integrated comp platform tied to retention metrics.
Retention comp metrics live; tied to dashboards.
AI-native comp engine recommends retention model tuning.

Data.text: Retention attribution data dirty or contested.
Attribution exists but disputed; manual reconciliation.
Retention attribution clean; single source of truth.
Retention attribution current and trusted; powers dashboards.
Data substrate enables continuous retention comp optimization.

levers.name: Governance
levers.description: Govern the program — forecast, risk, post-mortem.
levers.question: Can leadership govern retention with predictive intelligence — flagging trend reversals before they show up in reported NRR?

People.text: No named retention owner; metrics not trusted.
Informal retention owner; occasional reviews; static reports.
Clear retention owner; regular cadence; trusted dashboard.
Exec-level retention governance; segment owners drive numbers.
Board-visible retention program with predictive metrics.

Process.text: No cadence for retention reviews; metrics not reported.
Ad-hoc retention reviews; reports lag the action.
Regular retention cadence (forecast, risk, post-renewal); followed.
Exec retention cadence; integrated with broader business reviews.
Retention cadence partially autonomous; system surfaces issues.

Technology.text: No retention dashboards; metrics in spreadsheets after the fact.
Basic retention dashboards but stale or disputed.
Trusted retention dashboards exist and update reliably.
Live exec retention dashboards integrated across systems.
AI-native retention dashboards; predictive metrics flag reversals.

Data.text: Retention metrics not trusted; data dirty or disputed.
Some retention metrics tracked but disputed by stakeholders.
Retention metrics (GRR, at-risk $) trusted at department level.
Retention metrics trusted at exec level; current and joined.
Retention metrics trusted at board level; predictive.

People.text: Price set on cost-plus or competitor; no value anchoring.
Some reps reference value at quote time; inconsistent.
Reps and deal desk trained on value-based pricing.
Reps coached on value signals; segment owners drive pricing.

Pricing comp metrics live and current.
AI-native comp engine recommends pricing model tuning.

Data.text: Pricing attribution data dirty or contested.
Attribution exists but disputed; manual reconciliation.
Pricing attribution clean; single source of truth.
Attribution current and trusted; powers dashboards.
Data enables continuous pricing comp optimization.

levers.name: Governance
levers.description: Govern the program — forecast, risk, post-mortem.
levers.question: Can leadership govern pricing with predictive intelligence — flagging price-power shifts before they hit ARPA and effective discount?

People.text: No named pricing owner; pricing health invisible.
Informal pricing owner; ad-hoc reviews.
Clear pricing-strategy owner / deal-desk lead; cadence; dashboard.
Exec-level pricing committee; segment owners drive.
Board-visible pricing program with predictive metrics.

Process.text: No cadence for pricing reviews.
Ad-hoc pricing reviews; reports lag the action.
Regular pricing cadence (committee, governance).
Exec cadence; integrated with broader business reviews.
Cadence partially autonomous; system surfaces issues.

Technology.text: No pricing dashboards.
Basic pricing dashboards but stale or disputed.
Trusted pricing dashboards update reliably.
Live exec pricing dashboards integrated across systems.
AI-native pricing dashboards; predictive metrics flag shifts.

Data.text: Pricing metrics not trusted.
Some pricing metrics tracked but disputed.
Pricing metrics (ASP, discount) trusted at department level.
Pricing metrics trusted at exec level; current and joined.
Pricing metrics trusted at board level; predictive.
